Generated Reference

Generated from cli.py

CLI リファレンス

これは src/fslc/cli.py の argparse 定義から生成されています。各コマンドの使い方はPython自身の --help 出力そのものなので、手書きの一覧が実装から取り残されることはありません。

Exit codes & JSON envelope — exit_code() / _envelope() / _error_envelope(), from src/fslc/cli.py

Every command prints one JSON object to stdout and maps its result field to a process exit code through this function — verbatim from the source, so this table cannot drift from the actual contract:

def exit_code(result):
    r = result.get("result")
    if r in ("verified", "proved", "scenarios", "conformant", "generated",
             "refines", "typestate", "mutated", "explained", "analyzed",
             "verified_under_assumptions", "agent_analyzed", "replay_conformant",
             "observed_conformant", "imported", "imported_with_warnings",
             "expanded", "conformance_checked", "ai_project_analyzed",
             "statistically_supported", "observed_supported", "compared",
             "compat_profile_generated"):
        return 0
    if r == "sweep_passed":
        return 0
    if r in ("violated", "reachable_failed", "unknown_cti", "nonconformant",
             "refinement_failed", "sweep_failed", "replay_nonconformant",
             "observed_mismatch", "statistically_unsupported",
             "dataset_invalid", "evaluator_untrusted", "slice_missing",
             "insufficient_samples", "inconclusive"):
        return 1
    if r == "error":
        kind = result.get("kind")
        if kind == "internal":
            return 3
        return 2
    if r == "ok":
        return 0
    return 3

Every result is wrapped by _envelope() (adds {"fsl": "1.0", ...} + faithfulness metadata); parse/name/type/semantics/io errors additionally go through _error_envelope():

def _envelope(result):
    out = {"fsl": FSL_VERSION}
    out.update(result)
    out = with_faithfulness(out)
    tt = trace_type_for(out)
    if tt is not None:
        out.setdefault("trace_type", tt)
    return out


def _error_envelope(kind, message, loc=None, expected=None, hint=None):
    err = {"result": "error", "kind": kind, "message": message}
    if kind in ("parse", "name", "type", "semantics") and loc:
        err["loc"] = loc
    elif loc:
        err["loc"] = loc
    if expected:
        err["expected"] = expected
    if hint:
        err["hint"] = hint
    return _envelope(err)
fslc check
usage: fslc check [-h] [--strict-tags] [--requirements REQUIREMENTS] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --strict-tags
  --requirements REQUIREMENTS
fslc verify
usage: fslc verify [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--engine {bmc,induction}] [--k K_IND]
                   [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}]
                   [--vacuity {warn,error,ignore}] [--property PROPERTY_NAME]
                   [--exclude-property EXCLUDE_PROPERTY_NAMES]
                   [--instances INSTANCES] [--values VALUES] [--strict-tags]
                   [--requirements REQUIREMENTS] [--no-cache]
                   [--from-state FROM_STATE]
                   file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  --engine {bmc,induction}
  --k K_IND             max induction depth (induction engine only)
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}
  --vacuity {warn,error,ignore}
  --property PROPERTY_NAME
                        check a single named property in isolation; resolves
                        across invariant/trans/leadsTo/reachable declarations
  --exclude-property EXCLUDE_PROPERTY_NAMES
                        skip a named property; repeat to omit
                        invariants/trans/leadsTo/reachable declarations
  --instances INSTANCES
                        override a verify-block 'instances NAME = N' bound
                        (NAME=N; repeatable) — e.g. shrink to 1 entity for
                        liveness
  --values VALUES       override a verify-block 'values NAME = LO..HI' bound
                        (NAME=LO..HI; repeatable)
  --strict-tags
  --requirements REQUIREMENTS
  --no-cache            skip the persistent verdict cache for this run
                        (neither reads nor writes it)
  --from-state FROM_STATE
                        replace spec init with a complete Monitor/replay
                        logical-state JSON snapshot (BMC only)
fslc sweep
usage: fslc sweep [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--engine {bmc,induction}] [--k K_IND]
                  [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}]
                  [--vacuity {warn,error,ignore}] [--property PROPERTY_NAME]
                  [--instances INSTANCES] [--values VALUES] [--strict-tags]
                  [--requirements REQUIREMENTS]
                  file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH         depth range LO..HI to sweep (default: 0..8)
  --engine {bmc,induction}
  --k K_IND             max induction depth (induction engine only)
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}
  --vacuity {warn,error,ignore}
  --property PROPERTY_NAME
                        check a single named property in isolation
  --instances INSTANCES
                        sweep an entity bound (NAME=LO..HI; repeatable)
  --values VALUES       sweep a number upper bound as LO..LO through LO..HI
  --strict-tags
  --requirements REQUIREMENTS
fslc scenarios
usage: fslc scenarios [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}]
                      file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}
fslc replay
usage: fslc replay [-h] --trace TRACE file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help     show this help message and exit
  --trace TRACE
fslc testgen
usage: fslc testgen [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [-o OUTPUT]
                    [--target {pytest,vitest,swift,kotlin,dart,phpunit}]
                    [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}] [--strict]
                    file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  -o OUTPUT, --output OUTPUT
  --target {pytest,vitest,swift,kotlin,dart,phpunit}
                        test harness to emit (default: pytest)
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}
  --strict
fslc mutate
usage: fslc mutate [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--by-requirement]
                   [--max-mutants MAX_MUTANTS]
                   file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  --by-requirement
  --max-mutants MAX_MUTANTS
fslc explain
usage: fslc explain [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--readable] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help     show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  --readable
fslc html
usage: fslc html [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [-o OUTPUT]
                 [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}] [--engine {bmc,induction}]
                 file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  -o OUTPUT, --output OUTPUT
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}
  --engine {bmc,induction}
                        use k-induction so a proved report can show assurance
                        class 'proved'
fslc ledger
usage: fslc ledger [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [-o OUTPUT]
                   [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}] [--impl-log IMPL_LOG]
                   [--engine {bmc,induction}] [--evidence EVIDENCE]
                   file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  -o OUTPUT, --output OUTPUT
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}
  --impl-log IMPL_LOG   optional implementation trace JSON to score
                        conformance (fslc replay)
  --engine {bmc,induction}
                        use k-induction so a requirement's assurance class can
                        reach 'proved' (#171)
  --evidence EVIDENCE   path to a saved stdout envelope (JSON) from an
                        external-evidence producer (fslc ai eval/replay/drift,
                        fslc db observe, fslc domain replay, ...); repeatable
fslc refine
usage: fslc refine [-h] [--depth DEPTH] impl abs mapping [rest ...]

positional arguments:
  impl
  abs
  mapping
  rest           chain check: appending more (abs map) pairs runs an end-to-
                 end composed check

options:
  -h, --help     show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
fslc chain
usage: fslc chain [-h] [--keep-going] [path]

positional arguments:
  path

options:
  -h, --help    show this help message and exit
  --keep-going
fslc typestate
usage: fslc typestate [-h] [--ts] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit
  --ts        emit only the derivable entities' TypeScript to stdout instead
              of JSON
fslc analyze
usage: fslc analyze [-h]
                    [--projection {tsg,action_dependency_graph,action_state_graph,impact_graph,property_state_graph,requirement_property_graph,refinement_graph,traceability_graph}]
                    [--profile {ai-review}] [--focus FOCUS]
                    [--format {dot,json,mermaid}]
                    file [file ...]

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --projection {tsg,action_dependency_graph,action_state_graph,impact_graph,property_state_graph,requirement_property_graph,refinement_graph,traceability_graph}
                        structural projection to emit
  --profile {ai-review}
                        emit AI-readable structural review findings
  --focus FOCUS         node id for --projection impact_graph, e.g.
                        state:stock or action:checkout
  --format {dot,json,mermaid}

usage: fslc db check [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--engine {bmc,induction}]
                     [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}]
                     file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit

  --engine {bmc,induction}
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}

positional arguments:

options:

  --trace TRACE
fslc db import
usage: fslc db import [-h] [--name NAME] [--source {auto,sql,prisma}]
                      [-o OUTPUT]
                      file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --name NAME
  --source {auto,sql,prisma}
                        source format to import (default: auto by extension)
  -o OUTPUT, --output OUTPUT

options:
  -h, --help    show this help message and exit

fslc ai replay
usage: fslc ai replay [-h] --logs LOGS [--component COMPONENT] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --logs LOGS
  --component COMPONENT
                        ai_component to replay when FILE is a project-level
                        fsl-ai file
fslc ai eval
usage: fslc ai eval [-h] [--records RECORDS] [--dataset DATASET]
                    [--slice SLICE_NAME] [--property PROPERTY_NAME]
                    file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --records RECORDS
  --dataset DATASET
  --slice SLICE_NAME
  --property PROPERTY_NAME
fslc ai regress
usage: fslc ai regress [-h] [--migration MIGRATION] --before-records
                       BEFORE_RECORDS --after-records AFTER_RECORDS
                       [--dataset DATASET]
                       file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --migration MIGRATION
  --before-records BEFORE_RECORDS
  --after-records AFTER_RECORDS
  --dataset DATASET
fslc ai compare
usage: fslc ai compare [-h] --from FROM_RECORDS --to TO_RECORDS
                       [--from-label FROM_LABEL] [--to-label TO_LABEL]
                       [--dataset DATASET]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --from FROM_RECORDS
  --to TO_RECORDS
  --from-label FROM_LABEL
  --to-label TO_LABEL
  --dataset DATASET
fslc ai drift
usage: fslc ai drift [-h] --logs LOGS [--baseline-logs BASELINE_LOGS]
                     [--window WINDOW] [--baseline BASELINE]
                     [--property PROPERTY_NAME]
                     file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --logs LOGS
  --baseline-logs BASELINE_LOGS
  --window WINDOW
  --baseline BASELINE
  --property PROPERTY_NAME
